title.name: Denis Kukobin
title.headline: QA Lead — turning release chaos into predictable quality.

description.body: I lead QA on a casino and sportsbook platform: 21 microservices, a Go backend, a Next.js frontend, events over Kafka, observability in Grafana, and a move to Kubernetes. I push shift-left, Playwright automation, and processes the team actually uses instead of filing away. I like breaking the system before production — so there's nothing left to break in production.

items: Started in manual testing — learned to break a product before its users do.
Moved into automation: Playwright, API checks, and stable pipelines instead of manual grind.
Dove into a high-load casino and sportsbook platform — 21 microservices, Go, Kafka, Grafana.
Grew into process and people: building QA standards, leading teams, and driving shift-left.
Believe quality isn't a department at the end of the line, but a habit of the whole team.

items.detail: Manual testing, bug reports, checklists. Learned to think like a user and an attacker at once.
Test design, regression, working from requirements. Started catching defects at acceptance, not in prod.
Playwright automation, API checks, and CI integration. The grind moved into pipelines.
High-load product, microservices, Kafka, log analysis in Grafana. Tough incidents and investigations.
Leading teams, building QA processes and standards, driving shift-left and automation across the platform.
